target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent compaction overrides so each configured agent can choose its own compaction runtime behavior instead of inheriting every compaction decision from `agents.defaults.compaction`.

This is intended for multi-agent installations where the main assistant, channel-specific agents, local/self-hosted agents, and Codex/app-server workers have different runtime and transcript-management requirements.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related issues:

- #52751
- #69988
- #19501
- #15826
- #43834
- #6571
- #6572
- #30452
- #57901
- #58077
- #59863
- #60512
- #80784
- #82008

Related review/regression context addressed in this PR:

- strict `AgentEntrySchema` support for `agents.list[].compaction`
- defaults-only consumers for memory/notice/post-compaction behavior
- queued/direct compaction scope for legacy-session, non-main-agent runs

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, the config surface either rejected or only partially honored per-agent compaction settings. Several runtime paths still read from `agents.defaults.compaction` even when a specific `agents.list[]` entry defined its own compaction block.

That made these setups difficult or impossible to express correctly:

- main/default agent compacts with one provider/model
- channel or proxy agents compact with another provider/model
- local/self-hosted agents use a local provider alias for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit an OpenClaw compaction model override and keep their own runtime behavior
- non-main agents with legacy/non-agent session keys still need their active agent scope preserved during queued/direct compaction

## What Changed

This PR makes the per-agent compaction contract real end to end:

- extends the strict agent schema to accept `agents.list[].compaction`
- documents and labels the per-agent compaction fields
- resolves compaction provider/model/auth/profile choices through the active agent scope
- applies per-agent compaction settings in direct and queued compaction paths
- carries active `agentId` through preflight memory compaction, manual `/compact`, gateway session compaction, queued compaction, and direct compaction
- honors per-agent settings for:
  - `model`
  - `provider`
  - `authProfileId`
  - `timeoutSeconds`
  - `midTurnPrecheck`
  - `keepRecentTokens`
  - `truncateAfterCompaction`
  - `postIndexSync`
  - compaction memory-flush behavior
  - compaction follow-up/notice behavior
- keeps Codex/app-server agents able to omit a compaction model override so their native runtime path is not accidentally replaced by OpenClaw compaction selection
- adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent compaction scope in both direct and queued compaction

## Reviewer Notes

The important behavioral invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR passes `agentId` explicitly into compaction params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

The schema accepts the full per-agent compaction block, but this PR does not narrow the feature surface to schema-only support. The remaining defaults-only runtime consumers found during review were fixed so the accepted configuration is actually honored.

Scope audit result: after rechecking the PR surface, ancillary churn was removed from `src/config/model-refs.ts`, `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/VoiceWakeSettings.swift`, import ordering, oxlint-suppression namespace cleanup, and unrelated `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies` help wording. The subagent model-selection-only guard was restored in `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts` because it remains valid and protects the existing `subagents.model` contract.

The only intentionally retained non-feature test hygiene is `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts`, which clears the plugin metadata snapshot memo in teardown. This is included because removing it makes the full runtime-config suite fail order-dependently: `keeps shipped plugin install config records when index migration fails` receives no stale-plugin warning after earlier memoized plugin metadata leaks between tests. This does not change product behavior; it keeps the validation gate deterministic while this PR touches runtime config/schema surfaces.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Per-agent compaction overrides must resolve from the active agent scope instead of always inheriting `agents.defaults.compaction`, including legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys that require an explicit non-main `agentId`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on the PR branch plus a live OpenClaw gateway on `OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (50a2481)`, Node `v24.15.0`, host `Nano-01`, Discord channel binding for `#gb10`, native Codex app-server runtime authenticated through OAuth. Runtime proof logging was temporarily enabled at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/logs/gateway-debug-compaction-proof.log` with secrets redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a live source-module proof with `HEAD_SHA=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d NODE_COMPILE_CACHE= node --import tsx .artifacts/per-agent-compaction-proof.mjs`; then verified the deployed multi-agent config on a real gateway and triggered manual `/compact` in the real Discord `#gb10` channel.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript and redacted runtime log excerpt from the real setup:

```text
$ HEAD_SHA=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d NODE_COMPILE_CACHE= node --import tsx .artifacts/per-agent-compaction-proof.mjs
OpenClaw per-agent compaction live source proof
head=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d

explicitAgentId=lossless-agent
resolvedSessionAgentId=lossless-agent
compactionProvider=google

```

2026-05-18T22:28:31.302-07:00 INFO  completed codex app-server compaction sessionId=00109ec6-e729-4d21-9811-8294ad845acb
```
- Observed result after fix: The source-module proof selected the explicit non-main per-agent compaction override values `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `45000` ms timeout, and transcript rotation enabled for a legacy session key. The live deployed OpenClaw run accepted distinct per-agent compaction config blocks and completed a real Discord `/compact` in `#gb10`; the persisted session shows `compactionCount: 1`, `status: "done"`, and a live Codex app-server sidecar thread updated during the run.
- What was not tested: The live deployed Codex app-server compaction log does not currently print the resolved per-agent compaction model, so the live `#gb10` run is supplemental runtime proof while the source-module proof is the direct model/provider/timeout selection proof. Existing pre-restart Codex app-server sessions can also fail manual `/compact` with stale/missing native thread IDs; that appears to be a separate restart/session-recovery behavior and is not claimed fixed here.

## Supplemental Proof / Validation

Focused regression and runtime-scope tests:

```text

 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts \
 src/auto-reply/reply/memory-flush.test.ts \
 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts \
 src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts
```

target.title: feat(config): use datetime suffix for backup rotation instead of numeric
target.body:
Replace numeric backup rotation (`.bak.1`, `.bak.2`, …) with datetime-stamped suffixes (`.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHmmss` in UTC) so users can identify known-good backups at a glance without checking file modification times.

Closes #39923

## Summary

- **`rotateConfigBackups()`** — renames `.bak` → `.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHmmss` instead of shifting numbered slots
- **`cleanOrphanBackups()`** — keeps the N-1 most recent datetime-stamped backups (lexicographic sort = chronological); removes legacy numbered files (`.bak.1`, `.bak.2`) and other orphans on first upgrade run
- **`hardenBackupPermissions()`** — discovers datetime-stamped files via `readdir` instead of iterating a fixed numeric range
- **Collision handling** — appends `-2`, `-3`, … suffix for sub-second collisions
- **Backward compatible** — legacy `.bak.1`–`.bak.4` files are cleaned up automatically on first maintenance cycle

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / enhancement

## Test plan

- [x] All 8 tests pass (`vitest run src/config/config.backup-rotation.test.ts`) — 7 passed, 1 skipped (chmod on Windows)
- [x] New tests cover: datetime rotation, 5-deep ring enforcement, legacy numbered file cleanup, capacity limit pruning, collision suffix format validation, `isDatetimeSuffix()` unit tests
- [x] `maintainConfigBackups` compose test updated for datetime-stamped slots

## AI Disclosure

- [x] AI-assisted (Claude Opus 4.6 via Claude Code)
- [x] Fully tested
- [x] I understand what the code does
- [x] Will resolve bot review conversations after addressing them

target.title: fix(discord): remove invalid message_reference from webhook payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- Remove `message_reference` from Discord webhook execute payloads
- The [Execute Webhook](https://docs.discord.com/developers/resources/webhook) endpoint does not support this field; it is only valid on the [Create Message](https://docs.discord.com/developers/resources/message) endpoint (bot-token)
- The field was silently ignored by Discord, but sending unsupported fields risks future rejection

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify webhook sends still succeed without `message_reference`
- [ ] Confirm bot-token reply behavior (`replyTo` via `sendMessageDiscord`) is unaffected

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

target.title: fix(agents): only seed HEARTBEAT.md on brand-new workspaces
target.body:
## Problem `ensureAgentWorkspace()` creates HEARTBEAT.md for every workspace missing the file — including existing workspaces where the user intentionally removed it. The empty template was designed as a heartbeat blocker for fresh installs; without it, the heartbeat runs without guidance and the agent is left to figure out what to do on its own. This was introduced in #12027 which removed HEARTBEAT.md creation entirely. The intended behavior (from #11807) is to only create it on brand-new workspaces. ## Fix Gate `writeFileIfMissing(heartbeatPath, ...)` behind the existing `isBrandNewWorkspace` check. Only genuinely new workspaces receive the template. Existing workspaces that deliberately removed the file no longer have it recreated on every session start. ## Tests Two new e2e tests in `workspace.e2e.test.ts`: - `creates HEARTBEAT.md for brand-new workspaces` — verifies the template is still seeded for fresh installs - `does not create HEARTBEAT.md for existing workspaces that lack it` — verifies the file is not recreated when a user removed it All 10 workspace tests pass. Fixes #16065

Greptile Summary

This PR correctly fixes a regression where `HEARTBEAT.md` was being recreated for every workspace that lacked the file — including existing workspaces where the user had intentionally removed it. The fix is minimal and well-targeted: `writeFileIfMissing(heartbeatPath, heartbeatTemplate)` is now guarded by the existing `isBrandNewWorkspace` check, consistent with how `ensureGitRepo` is already gated. **Key changes:** - `src/agents/workspace.ts`: `HEARTBEAT.md` creation is now inside `if (isBrandNewWorkspace)` (line 317). The underlying `isBrandNewWorkspace` computation already includes `heartbeatPath` in its six-file check (line 289), so a workspace where all other files exist but `HEARTBEAT.md` was deleted will correctly evaluate to `false` — the fix is logically sound. - `src/agents/workspace.e2e.test.ts`: Two new tests added — one confirming `HEARTBEAT.md` is seeded on fresh installs, one confirming it isn't recreated on existing workspaces. The second test's setup only pre-writes two of the six core files, which means it doesn't precisely model the described scenario (a fully-initialized workspace that had `HEARTBEAT.md` removed), though it still exercises the correct code path. - One minor style note: `heartbeatTemplate` is unconditionally loaded (line 308) even when `isBrandNewWorkspace` is `false`, though this is low-impact given the in-process template cache.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge; the change is a narrow, well-scoped guard around an existing code path with no risk of data loss or behavioral regression. - The fix is a one-line conditional guard that only restricts behavior (prevents HEARTBEAT.md recreation on existing workspaces) — it cannot break existing workspaces. The `isBrandNewWorkspace` check already included `heartbeatPath`, ensuring the logic handles the user-deleted-HEARTBEAT.md case correctly. Two new e2e tests cover both the positive and negative path. The test for the "existing workspace" scenario is slightly under-specified but still validates the correct outcome. - No files require special attention; the test setup in `workspace.e2e.test.ts` for the "existing workspace" scenario could be more precise but does not affect correctness. Last reviewed commit: acc511a

target.title: Fix: preserve modelOverride in agent handler (#5369)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the intermittent race condition where sub-agent model overrides were silently ignored (~3% failure rate).

- When `sessions.patch` writes a fresh `modelOverride` between when the agent handler captures its local `entry` reference and when it builds `nextEntryPatch`, the captured stale value would clobber the fresh store value through `mergeSessionEntry({...freshStore, ...patch})`.
- The fix removes the stale `entry?.X` writebacks from `nextEntryPatch` (incl. `modelOverride`, `providerOverride`, `cliSessionIds`, `cliSessionBindings`, `claudeCliSessionId`, gated `pluginOwnerId`). `mergeSessionEntry` already preserves anything not in the patch, so fresh store values survive the write.
- Rotation-clearing for `sessionFile` (added by #79076) is preserved through a conditional spread that only writes `sessionFile: undefined` on session rotation.

## Root Cause

The agent handler was:
1. Reading the session entry early via `loadSessionEntry()` and holding it in a local variable.
2. Building `nextEntryPatch` with `modelOverride: entry?.modelOverride` and several siblings — values read from that captured local entry, not the fresh store.
3. Calling `updateSessionStore()` whose mutator does `mergeSessionEntry(store[primaryKey], nextEntryPatch)` — which spreads `{...freshStore, ...patch}`, so any stale value in the patch clobbers the fresh store value written concurrently (e.g., by `sessions.patch` setting `modelOverride` on a sub-agent between spawn and run).

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`: drop stale `entry?.X` writebacks from `nextEntryPatch`. Switch the patch type to `Partial<SessionEntry>`. Gate `pluginOwnerId` behind `entry === undefined`. Convert the `sessionFile` rotation-clearing from #79076 into a conditional spread that fires only when `entry.sessionId !== sessionId`.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`: add the original #5369 regression and a broader regression guard for the whole stale-writeback class (sendPolicy, skillsSnapshot, thinkingLevel, fastMode, verboseLevel, traceLevel, reasoningLevel, systemSent, spawnedWorkspaceDir, spawnDepth, cliSessionIds, cliSessionBindings, claudeCliSessionId).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When `sessions.patch` writes a fresh `modelOverride` between when the agent handler captures its local `entry` reference and when it builds `nextEntryPatch`, the captured stale value clobbers the fresh store value through `mergeSessionEntry({...freshStore, ...patch})`. Per #5369, this manifested as ~3% of sub-agent runs silently ignoring an explicit model override and falling back to the parent's model.

**Real environment tested:**
- Host: Linux 6.14.0-34-generic on x86_64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw production session-store modules from this branch (`src/config/sessions/store.ts`, `src/config/sessions/types.ts`).
- Multipass guest VM `ai-assistant`: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, IP 10.119.193.216, Node v22.22.2, same modules from this branch via a host mount at `/home/ubuntu/openclaw-pr5369`.

Both run the production `loadSessionStore` / `updateSessionStore` / `mergeSessionEntry` code paths against a real on-disk JSON store in a per-run tmp directory. No mocking. No shims.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
node --import tsx scripts/repro-5369.mts 1000
```

The driver lives in this branch (local-only, intentionally not committed). For each of 1000 iterations it:

1. Initializes a session entry on disk via `updateSessionStore` (no override yet).
2. Captures the entry via `loadSessionStore({ skipCache: true })` — the same shape the agent handler holds in its local `entry` variable.
3. Fires a concurrent `updateSessionStore` to set `modelOverride: haiku-4.5-iter-N` (mimicking what `sessions.patch` does during sub-agent setup).
4. Builds `nextEntryPatch` two ways and applies each through the same `mergeSessionEntry(store[primaryKey], nextEntryPatch)` call shape that `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` uses:
   - PRE-FIX shape: `modelOverride: capturedEntry.modelOverride` (writes back the captured stale `undefined`).
   - POST-FIX shape: `modelOverride` dropped from the patch — `mergeSessionEntry` preserves the fresh store value.
5. Reads the persisted entry back from disk and reports whether the override survived.

**Evidence after fix:**

Host (Linux 6.14, Node v22.22.0):

```
[repro #5369] node v22.22.0 on linux x64
[repro #5369] storePath=/tmp/openclaw-pr5369-0fZPH1/sessions.json
[repro #5369] iterations=1000

[repro #5369] running PRE-FIX patch shape (entry?.modelOverride writeback)...
[repro #5369] PRE-FIX :  1000/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (338 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=undefined

[repro #5369] running POST-FIX patch shape (modelOverride dropped from patch)...
[repro #5369] POST-FIX:  0/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (254 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0

[repro #5369] verdict: fix VERIFIED
```

Multipass guest VM `ai-assistant` (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Node v22.22.2):

```
[repro #5369] node v22.22.2 on linux x64
[repro #5369] storePath=/tmp/openclaw-pr5369-4EJXTF/sessions.json
[repro #5369] iterations=1000

[repro #5369] running PRE-FIX patch shape (entry?.modelOverride writeback)...
[repro #5369] PRE-FIX :  1000/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (2602 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=undefined

[repro #5369] running POST-FIX patch shape (modelOverride dropped from patch)...
[repro #5369] POST-FIX:  0/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (1337 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0

[repro #5369] verdict: fix VERIFIED
```

**Observed result after fix:** in both environments every one of 1000 iterations preserved the sub-agent `modelOverride` written by the concurrent store update. The same 1000 iterations under the pre-fix patch shape lost the override every single time — confirming the writeback was the clobbering vector and that dropping it from `nextEntryPatch` is what makes the override durable. The persisted on-disk values (`actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0`) match what `sessions.patch` wrote, end-to-end, through the production merge code.

The 100% pre-fix loss rate (vs the ~3% reported in #5369) reflects that the driver deterministically forces the stale-then-fresh ordering on every iteration. Production traffic only hits that ordering when `sessions.patch` lands in the narrow window between the agent handler's `loadSessionEntry` and its own `updateSessionStore` call — about 3% of the time per the linked issue. Whenever that ordering does occur in production, the driver shows the pre-fix shape always loses the override and the post-fix shape never does.

**What was not tested:** end-to-end Anthropic API calls burning credit on each of the 1000 iterations. The sub-agent dispatch loop is upstream of the `modelOverride` persistence the fix addresses; a real model loop would only confirm what the persisted `modelOverride` value is, which the driver already reads back from disk on every iteration. Driving 1000 real Anthropic calls would not provide additional information about the persistence behavior the fix changes.

Fixes #5369

Supersedes #8398 (rebased onto latest main to resolve conflicts).

target.title: fix(cron): capture originating session/agent on the cron wake tool call
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** The cron `wake` MCP tool forwards only `{mode, text}` to the gateway, so every wake enqueues against the heartbeat/main default. Wakes scheduled from a non-main session (Telegram thread, Discord channel, multi-agent setup) silently route to the wrong conversation lane — on CLI runtimes the fired wake spawns a fresh session and burns tokens generating output nothing can route back; on embedded the wake fires the agent's default session, not the originating one.
- **What changed.** Wake tool resolves `opts.agentSessionKey` via the existing `resolveInternalSessionKey` / `resolveSessionAgentId` pattern (mirrors the `add` action at L569-583) and threads `sessionKey` + `agentId` through `WakeParamsSchema` → gateway handler → `context.cron.wake` → `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeat`. Explicit params on the tool call take precedence over inferred values.
- **CLI runtime coverage (claude-cli + claude-cli-interactive).** No CLI-specific changes — the runner's `useResume` is already auto-derived from `cliSessionId` + `backend.resumeArgs` presence (`src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:276-277`). Once the wake's `sessionKey` reaches the cron service, the heartbeat-runner targets the right session lane → the cron isolated agent resolves the right `sessionEntry` → `getCliSessionId` returns that session's claude-side id → `--resume <sessionId>` flips on automatically for any backend that declares `resumeArgs` (both Claude backends do). Same path works for Codex, Gemini-CLI, etc.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** `WakeParamsSchema` still permits `additionalProperties: true`. Pre-existing call sites that omit origin still route to the dep's default (backwards compatible). Subagent session keys reject early with `unwakeable-session-key` (upstream guard, preserved). Channel/thread/topic capture on the job's `delivery` block stays a follow-up — this PR's session-key bridge unblocks the wake-side routing without touching the delivery contract.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** cron `wake` invoked by an agent inside a non-main session threads the originating session/agent through the wake gateway call so the system event lands on that session's queue and the targeted heartbeat picks it up. CLI runtimes (both `claude-cli` headless and `claude-cli-interactive`) consume the resolved session through the existing `useResume` auto-derivation, so the woken claude process resumes the originating conversation rather than spawning fresh.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, AJV-compiled TypeBox validators, vitest 4.1.6. Live harness in `scripts/proof-cron-wake-origin.mts` drives the patched gateway wake handler (`validateWakeParams` + `isSubagentSessionKey` guard) through the patched `cron.wake()` / `timer.ts` with logging deps that print every `enqueueSystemEvent` / `requestHeartbeat` call.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/wake-origin.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts && pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-cron-wake-origin.mts`.
- **Evidence after fix:** unit-test suite — `Test Files 3 passed (3) / Tests 82 passed (82) / Duration 8.05s`. Live harness stdout (synthetic ids; real chat identifiers redacted at source — the script never references real ones):

  ```
  === non-main session + non-default agent (the bug-fix case) ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"follow up on report","sessionKey":"agent:coding:telegram:<chat-id-redacted>:topic:<topic-id-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="follow up on report"  opts={"sessionKey":"agent:coding:telegram:<chat-id-redacted>:topic:<topic-id-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake","sessionKey":"agent:coding:telegram:<chat-id-redacted>:topic:<topic-id-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}

  === no origin (backwards-compatible default routing) ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"ping"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="ping"  opts=undefined
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}

  === next-heartbeat + sessionKey collapses to targeted-immediate ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"next-heartbeat","text":"check the queue","sessionKey":"agent:coding:discord:<thread-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="check the queue"  opts={"sessionKey":"agent:coding:discord:<thread-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake","sessionKey":"agent:coding:discord:<thread-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}

  === subagent sessionKey rejected by gateway handler guard ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"wake my subagent","sessionKey":"subagent:scratch:<id-redacted>"}
  < wake result:  ok=false  payload=undefined
  < wake error:   {"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"wake sessionKey cannot target a subagent session"}

  === whitespace-only origin falls through (defence-in-depth) ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"x","sessionKey":"   ","agentId":"\t"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="x"  opts=undefined
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** non-main wake calls hit `enqueueSystemEvent(text, {sessionKey, agentId})` and `requestHeartbeat({source: "manual", intent: "immediate", reason: "wake", sessionKey, agentId})` on the originating lane, not the heartbeat/main default. No-origin calls preserve the pre-fix `enqueueSystemEvent(text, undefined)` + `requestHeartbeat({source, intent, reason})` shape. `mode: "next-heartbeat" + sessionKey` collapses to targeted-immediate because the regularly-scheduled heartbeat fires for the agent's main session, never peeking the targeted lane's queue. Subagent session keys reject at the gateway handler with `INVALID_REQUEST`. Whitespace-only origin trims to undefined and falls through to default routing (defence-in-depth at both the handler and `wake()`).
- **What was not tested:** live end-to-end Telegram thread → cron wake → CLI claude run → reply landing back in the thread. The harness pins the gateway-side routing contract; the CLI `useResume` auto-derivation is exercised by existing CLI runner tests on `--resume` argv assembly. A full live-Telegram capture is a Mantis follow-up (it needs `delivery.{channel,threadId,topicId}` capture on the cron job, which is deliberately split into a separate PR per the scope boundary above).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Closes the upstream half of #46886 and #64556.
- Related (downstream slices): #70268, #57199, #82767, #79869, #63096. This PR fixes the upstream origin-capture they all silently assume.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** existing in-process callers of `wake({mode, text})` keep working only if origin remains optional.
  - **Mitigation:** `sessionKey` / `agentId` optional; omitted (or whitespace-only) falls through to the dep's default. Regression test pins both branches.
- **Risk:** new `NonEmptyString` slots on the schema reject empty-string origin from existing callers.
  - **Mitigation:** existing callers don't send these fields. `additionalProperties: true` unchanged so forward-compat metadata still passes.
- **Risk:** inferred `agentId` could differ from a wake intended for a different agent (e.g. operator scheduling a wake for `coding` from `main`).
  - **Mitigation:** explicit `sessionKey` / `agentId` params on the tool call override the inferred values. The implicit capture is the default for the "wake my own session" case.
- **Risk:** a CLI-backend wake that doesn't have a prior claude session on disk (`cliSessionId === undefined`) would skip the `--resume` plumbing and start fresh.
  - **Mitigation:** that's the correct behaviour — fresh-session wakes have no history to resume. The auto-derivation gate (`useResume = Boolean(cliSessionId && resolvedSessionId && backend.resumeArgs)`) means existing sessions resume and brand-new sessions don't try to resume a session that doesn't exist.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — session keys and agent ids are not credentials.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `wake` action; only the routing target changes.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — wake events still confined to in-process session queues; routing now goes to the correct queue.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched. `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: fix(sandbox): make no-new-privileges configurable
target.body:
Add sandbox.docker.noNewPrivileges with a secure default of true, so hosts that reject Docker no-new-privileges can opt out explicitly without patching dist output by hand.

Also add tests, docs, and dangerous-config reporting for the security-weaker noNewPrivileges=false override.

Fixes #43996

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: configure multiple gateway agents with usage-bearing transcripts outside the default agent, then compare unscoped `usage.cost` with `sessions.usage`; current main fans out `sessions.usage` but leaves `usage.cost` on the default cost scope.

target.title: fix(gateway): aggregate usage cost across agents
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets: - Problem: `usage.cost` scanned only one default transcript directory when callers did not pass `agentId`, while `sessions.usage` already discovered sessions across configured agents. - Why it matters: Control UI and menu usage views can show empty or partial daily cost data in multi-agent deployments even when session usage exists under other agents. - What changed: resolve the `usage.cost` agent scope from configured gateway agents, merge daily/totals across agents, include the resolved scope in the cache key, and preserve date-only daily labels in the Control UI. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not implement the dedicated `usage.jsonl` storage proposal from #13219 or a separate cost-unit normalization pass; it addresses the confirmed cross-agent aggregation/scope mismatch reported in #41986. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #13219 - Addresses the confirmed aggregation-scope bug from #41986 - Supersedes #42063 by @stablegenius49; this PR carries forward its core `usage.cost` aggregation direction with additional cache-scope, explicit-agent, UI date-label, and partial-failure coverage. - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: `usage.cost` called `loadCostUsageSummary` without an agent scope, which resolves through the single-agent/default transcript directory instead of the configured gateway agent set. - Missing detection / guardrail: gateway tests covered multi-agent discovery for `sessions.usage`, but not the sibling `usage.cost` aggregate path, non-`main` default agent ids, or browser-visible daily usage rendering for date-only buckets. - Contributing context (if known): #13219 describes a broader usage tracking enhancement; this is a narrower current-state aggregate fix. #42063 previously proposed the core aggregation direction, and this PR supersedes it with broader scope validation, cache scoping, UI, and partial-failure coverage. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: `usage.cost` without `agentId` calls the cost summary loader once per configured agent and merges daily/totals; explicit `agentId` remains scoped; configured default agents do not need to be named `main`; date-only daily buckets render with the same calendar day in the Control UI. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the gateway handler boundary, mocked transcript-cost seam, and UI date formatting behavior without needing real transcript files. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for multi-agent `usage.cost`. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Multi-agent deployments should see `usage.cost` totals and daily entries aggregated across configured agents when no `agentId` is requested. Explicit `agentId` requests remain scoped to that agent. The Control UI daily usage chart should no longer show `No data` when cross-agent session usage exists, and date-only daily buckets no longer shift one day backward for users west of UTC. ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text Before: Control UI -> usage.cost(no agentId) -> one default transcript directory only -> Daily Usage: No data After: Control UI -> usage.cost(no agentId) -> configured agents -> merged daily/totals -> Daily Token Usage ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? Yes - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `usage.cost` now reads the same configured agent session scopes that `sessions.usage` already reads. Explicit `agentId` requests are validated and remain scoped, and cache keys include the resolved agent scope. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: host Node `v25.9.0`, pnpm `10.33.0` - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Gateway usage RPC and Control UI - Relevant config (redacted): `agents.list = [{ id: "ops", default: true }, { id: "opus" }]` ### Steps 1. Configure multiple agents with usage-bearing transcripts outside a single default transcript directory. 2. Request `usage.cost` without `agentId`. 3. Compare returned daily/totals with per-agent cost summaries and the Control UI Usage page. ### Expected - The response includes merged daily/totals across configured agents. - The Control UI daily usage chart renders the merged daily bucket instead of `No data`. ### Actual - Before this change, only one default transcript directory was scanned, so the Control UI could show `Daily Usage` / `No data` while `sessions.usage` showed activity. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Trace/log summary: - Before/live gateway `18789`: `usage.cost` for `2026-04-22` returned `daily: []` and zero totals, while `sessions.usage` for the same range returned a populated daily aggregate. - After/PR gateway `18790`: `usage.cost` for `2026-04-22` returned a populated daily bucket with about `117M` tokens and by-type breakdown. - Chrome MCP visual smoke on the PR gateway showed `Daily Token Usage`, `Apr 22`, `April 22, 2026`, and token-type breakdown rows instead of `No data`. - Chrome MCP visual smoke also verified the `Cost` toggle before/after: the official gateway still showed the daily chart as `No data`, while the PR gateway rendered `Daily Cost`, `Apr 22`, `$111.24`, and `COST BY TYPE`. - Screenshots are embedded in this PR description and are not committed to the codebase. Screenshots: Before, current official build showing the scrolled `Daily Usage` section with `No data`: After, PR gateway `18790` showing the scrolled `Daily Token Usage` section populated for `Apr 22`: Before, current official build with `Cost` selected still showing the daily chart as `No data`: After, PR gateway `18790` with `Cost` selected showing `Daily Cost` populated for `Apr 22`: ## Prior Work / Credit This PR supersedes #42063 by @stablegenius49 and carries forward its core direction: make `usage.cost` aggregate across configured gateway agents. The implementation here expands that work with explicit `agentId` validation/scoping, agent-scoped cache keys, partial per-agent failure handling, non-`main` agent coverage, UI date-label handling, and visual verification. Suggested maintainer landing credit, if squashing or direct-landing this PR: `(thanks @stablegenius49)` or `Co-authored-by: stablegenius49 <185121704+stablegenius49@users.noreply.github.com>`. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: multi-agent `usage.cost` aggregate, explicit `agentId` scoping, invalid/out-of-scope explicit `agentId` rejection, cache behavior with agent-scoped cache keys, configured default agent not named `main`, first configured agent fallback, no-agent fallback to `main`, discovered disk agents when no list constrains the gateway, Control UI daily token rendering, and Control UI daily cost rendering against a PR-branch gateway. - Edge cases checked: bounded cache eviction, in-flight cache coalescing, gateway typecheck/lint/import-cycle guards, date-only bucket rendering under `America/Sao_Paulo` timezone. - What you did **not** verify: no known unverified items for this PR scope. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: more transcript directories may be scanned for unscoped `usage.cost` requests. - Mitigation: the gateway already uses the same configured agent list for `sessions.usage`, and `usage.cost` remains cached with the resolved agent scope included in the cache key. ## AI Assistance - AI-assisted: Yes, implemented with OpenAI Codex. - Degree of testing: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, targeted gateway usage/UI Vitest shards (`pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/usage-helpers.node.test.ts test/official-channel-catalog.test.ts`), `pnpm check:changed`, `pnpm build`, Chrome MCP visual smoke against a PR-branch gateway including `Tokens` and `Cost` modes, and `codex review --base origin/main` at refreshed head `c613510233` with no actionable findings. Current mergeable head `e14c554f32` was refreshed with a normal merge from `origin/main` and revalidated with `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, the focused usage/UI tests, `pnpm check:changed`, and `pnpm build`. - Session logs/prompts: not attached. - Understanding: I reviewed the agent-scope and gateway usage paths; the production change aggregates the existing transcript-backed cost summaries across resolved gateway agent ids, preserves explicit `agentId` scoping, and fixes date-only daily label rendering. - Local Codex review: `codex review --base origin/main` was run locally and reported no actionable findings.

target.title: chore(observability): scope eslint/no-console rule to src with allowlist + mark FileReader listeners { once: true }
target.body:
## Summary

Additive observability hygiene — one new lint rule + a small listener cleanup clarity fix. No runtime behavior change.

**1. Scoped `eslint/no-console: error` rule**

Enabled for `src/**/*.{ts,tsx}` (excluding test files) in `.oxlintrc.json`. Prevents new `console.*` sites from landing in production paths without either (a) routing through the structured logger, or (b) adding an explicit allowlist entry.

Current-tree allowlist covers **38 files** with legitimate console use, grouped by reason:

- **CLI user-output** (intentional prints to stdout/stderr): `entry.ts`, `entry.version-fast-path.ts`, `cli/*`, `commands/health.ts`, `runtime.ts`, `globals.ts`, `logger.ts`, `terminal/restore.ts`, `acp/{client,server}.ts`, `infra/tailscale.ts`, `infra/dotenv.ts`.
- **Catastrophic / pre-logger fallbacks**: `infra/unhandled-rejections.ts`, `index.ts`, `infra/tmp-openclaw-dir.ts` (imminent-exit paths where the file logger may not be stable).
- **Logger internals**: `logging/console.ts`, `logging/subsystem.ts`, `logging/test-helpers/console-snapshot.ts`.
- **Default-fallback parameters**: `channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.ts`, `config/defaults.ts`.
- **Library noise suppression**: `infra/bonjour.ts` (monkey-patches `console.log` to silence ciao).
- **Scraper output contract**: `plugins/plugin-load-profile.ts`, `plugin-sdk/channel-entry-contract.ts` (profile probes emit a specific stderr format that tooling depends on per the module header).
- **Diagnostic paths covered by existing tests that spy on `console.*`** (not migrated here — follow-up migrations would need to update the test spies in the same change): `channels/typing.ts`, `context-engine/registry.ts`, `gateway/server-http.ts`, `auto-reply/command-auth.ts`, `infra/diagnostic-events.ts`.
- **Debug-gated diagnostics** (env-var-gated; migration would need a deliberate level choice — `.info` vs `.debug` — and is deferred): `gateway/mcp-http.{ts,request.ts}`, `gateway/gateway-cli-backend.live-helpers.ts`, `auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts`, `config/doc-baseline.ts`, `agents/agent-command.ts`, `infra/temp-download.ts`.
- **Pending CODEOWNERS review**: `security/windows-acl.ts` (has an in-file TODO asking for a structured-logger migration; under `@openclaw/secops` ownership).
- **Test-utility**: `test-utils/generation-live-test-helpers.ts`.

Tests (`**/*.test.ts` etc.) get `no-console: off` via a trailing override so the rule never flags test fixtures or console-spy tests.

**2. FileReader `{ once: true }` cleanup in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`**

Three `reader.addEventListener("load", ...)` call sites in the chat composer converted to `{ once: true }`. Not a leak fix — `FileReader` is GC'd after `readAsDataURL` completes anyway — but the explicit flag communicates intent and matches the rest of the codebase's listener-cleanup conventions.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm lint` — `no-console` enabled on `src/**`, 0 new errors across core + extensions lanes. (4 pre-existing `no-redundant-type-constituents` errors in `extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/aimock/server.ts` confirmed present on `origin/main`.)
- [x] Current-tree allowlist exactly covers every non-test `console.*` reference under `src/` on `origin/main`.

## Blast radius

- The rule is additive: existing code keeps working; new unjustified `console.*` in `src/**` will fail CI.
- Listener change is pure clarity — same observable behavior.

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- Migrate allowlisted diagnostic-path sites to the structured subsystem logger in per-owner follow-up PRs, updating the accompanying test spies in the same change (tests currently spy on `console.error`/`console.warn` as the contract).
- Decide `.info` vs `.debug` level for each env-var-gated debug path as part of those migrations.

target.title: fix(agents): use CJK-aware token estimation in compaction path (#70052)
target.body:
## Summary
The compaction path uses `estimateTokens` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` which assumes ~4 chars/token for all scripts. This severely underestimates CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) content where each character maps to ~1 token, causing compaction to trigger too late and leading to context window overflow.

## Root Cause
The upstream `estimateTokens()` counts `text.length / 4` for all scripts. For CJK text, this yields ~0.25 tokens/char instead of ~1 token/char (a 4x underestimation). OpenClaw already has a CJK-aware estimator (`estimateStringChars` in `src/utils/cjk-chars.ts`) used in the pruning path, but the compaction path was not using it.

clawsweeper's review on this issue confirmed the failing path and explicitly identified this PR as the "active fix PR" when the upstream issue was closed-as-duplicate: *"Source inspection shows current main uses the upstream `chars/4` estimator while the dependency contract confirms CJK text is counted as ~1 token/char"* and *"Close as superseded by the active fix PR #70112. Current main still uses the upstream `estimateTokens` in the reported compaction paths, so this is not implemented-on-main, but #70112 directly tracks and patches the same remaining gap."*

## Changes
- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: Add `cjkTokenCorrection()` helper and `estimateTokensCjkAware()` wrapper that adds CJK correction delta on top of the upstream estimate. Use it in `estimateMessagesTokens()`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts`: Replace all 4 `estimateTokens` references with `estimateTokensCjkAware` (metrics, full session estimation, hook callbacks).
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts`: Replace `estimateTokens` with `estimateTokensCjkAware` for synthetic message estimation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Compaction triggers too late on Korean / Chinese / Japanese conversations because `estimateTokens` (from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`) divides `text.length` by 4 for ALL scripts, but CJK text is roughly 1 token per character on real-world tokenizers. Result: a Korean conversation that actually consumes ~615 tokens of context is reported as ~195 tokens, so the runtime thinks the window is 3× emptier than reality and lets it overflow before compaction triggers. clawsweeper closed issue #70052 explicitly tagging this PR (#70112) as the active fix.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-70052` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14, executing the patched production exports from `src/agents/compaction.ts` (`estimateTokensCjkAware`, `estimateMessagesTokens`) and `src/utils/cjk-chars.ts` (`estimateStringChars`, `CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE`) directly through `node --import tsx`. No mocks, no test runner — the same production helpers the compaction path calls. PR head: `e5f7bf2d9dd55639d21bcc20b78a10e834f08301`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/cjk-token-estimation && node --import tsx ./smoke-pr70112.ts`, where `smoke-pr70112.ts` imports `estimateTokensCjkAware` / `estimateMessagesTokens` from `./src/agents/compaction.js` and builds three sample messages (Korean, Chinese, English) of comparable lengths to compare the upstream `chars/4` formula against the CJK-aware estimator and a control English message.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `e5f7bf2d9d` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/agents/compaction.ts` and `src/utils/cjk-chars.ts` exercised through their real exports via `node --import tsx`).

~~~text
$ node --import tsx ./smoke-pr70112.ts
=== Character / token reference values ===
CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE = 4

--- korean message ---
  rawLen (UTF-16)            = 780
  codePointLen               = 780
  estimateStringChars        = 2460
  upstream chars/4 estimate  = 195 tokens
  estimateTokensCjkAware     = 615 tokens
  inflation ratio (new/old)  = 3.15x

--- chinese message ---
  rawLen (UTF-16)            = 360
  codePointLen               = 360
  estimateStringChars        = 1395
  upstream chars/4 estimate  = 90 tokens
  estimateTokensCjkAware     = 259 tokens
  inflation ratio (new/old)  = 2.88x

--- english message ---
  rawLen (UTF-16)            = 1700
  codePointLen               = 1700
  estimateStringChars        = 1700
  upstream chars/4 estimate  = 425 tokens
  estimateTokensCjkAware     = 425 tokens
  inflation ratio (new/old)  = 1.00x

=== Conversation total via estimateMessagesTokens ===
  messages = 3
  totalTokens (CJK-aware) = 1299
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: For 780 UTF-16 chars of Korean content the upstream estimate is 195 tokens but the CJK-aware estimator returns **615 tokens** (3.15× inflation, matching the ~1 token/char reality for Korean syllables). For 360 chars of Chinese it returns 259 tokens (2.88× inflation). For 1700 chars of plain English the estimator returns **exactly the same 425 tokens** as before — confirming the correction is additive on top of the upstream estimate and Latin-only conversations are unaffected. `estimateMessagesTokens` over the mixed 3-message conversation reports 1299 tokens, the sum of the per-message CJK-aware estimates, demonstrating the fix reaches the actual compaction-trigger arithmetic.
- **What was not tested**: A full live `runEmbeddedPiAgent` cycle against a real LLM with a CJK transcript hitting the context window was not exercised on the contributor machine (would need an actual provider key and a multi-thousand-message Korean session). The fix is a pure-function character-counting change at the estimator layer; the four compaction call sites (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts`: metrics, full session estimation, hook callbacks) and the preemptive path (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts`) all swap `estimateTokens` → `estimateTokensCjkAware` with identical signatures, so the only runtime change is the (now-accurate) numeric estimate that drives the existing trigger threshold. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live LLM run with a CJK transcript is required.

## Test

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/compaction.test.ts` — 17 passed (existing + new CJK regression cases)
- `pnpm test -- src/utils/cjk-chars.test.ts` — 17 passed
- `pnpm test -- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts` — 9 passed

Closes #70052

target.title: fix(skills): add watcher polling fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the skills watcher only relied on native filesystem events, so skill changes could be missed on filesystems where those events are unreliable or absent.
- Why it matters: when a `SKILL.md` change is missed, sessions can keep a stale skills snapshot until some unrelated invalidation happens.
- What changed: added an opt-in `chokidar` polling fallback for the skills watcher via `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLLING` and `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS`, plus tests and docs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default watcher behavior stays event-driven, and refreshed skills still apply on the next turn rather than inside an in-flight run.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the skills watcher only depended on native file notifications from `chokidar`, with no fallback when the underlying filesystem failed to emit them reliably.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no opt-in polling mode for the skills watcher.
- Contributing context (if known): this came up while checking whether dynamically injected skills refresh without requiring a new session.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/skills/refresh.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: enabling `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLLING` passes `usePolling` to the watcher, and `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS` is forwarded only when valid.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression is in watcher construction, so unit coverage around `ensureSkillsWatcher()` is enough without booting the full gateway.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the existing watcher test in `src/agents/skills/refresh.test.ts` already covers watch targets and ignore behavior.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Users can now opt into polling for the skills watcher with `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLLING=1`.
- Users can optionally tune the polling interval with `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=<ms>`.
- Default behavior is unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[skill file changes] -> [native FS event missed] -> [skills snapshot version not bumped]

After:
[skill file changes] -> [polling enabled] -> [watcher notices change] -> [skills snapshot version bumped] -> [next turn rebuilds snapshot]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): optional env-only override for the skills watcher

### Steps

1. Set `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLLING=1`.
2. Optionally set `OPENCLAW_SKILLS_WATCH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=1000`.
3. Run the targeted watcher test and verify the watcher is configured with polling enabled.

### Expected

- The skills watcher supports polling fallback when explicitly enabled.
- Invalid polling interval values are ignored.
- Default behavior remains unchanged when the env vars are unset.

### Actual

- Targeted test passed with the new polling coverage.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted watcher test passed after the change; reviewed the watcher code path and confirmed it still bumps the skills snapshot and refreshes on the next turn.
- Edge cases checked: invalid polling interval env values are ignored; polling env is only applied when explicitly enabled.
- What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end run on a filesystem that actively drops native watcher events.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: polling can add extra filesystem overhead when enabled.
  - Mitigation: the fallback is opt-in only, and the interval is configurable.
- Risk: users may expect polling to refresh an already running turn.
  - Mitigation: docs keep the existing behavior explicit: refresh happens on the next turn, not mid-run.
```

target.title: [codex] suppress heartbeat-isolated exec self-wake loop
target.body:
## Summary

Suppress node-level `exec` system-event replay for synthetic heartbeat-isolated sessions.

When a heartbeat run is already executing inside a synthetic `:heartbeat` session, any internal `exec.started` / `exec.finished` / `exec.denied` node event should stay in that transcript only. Re-enqueuing the same event as a system event and immediately calling `requestHeartbeatNow(...)` creates a self-wake loop:

```text
heartbeat -> exec -> exec-event wake -> heartbeat
```

This PR adds a narrow guard in `server-node-events` so entries tagged with `heartbeatIsolatedBaseSessionKey` do not enqueue another system event or request another heartbeat wake.

## Root cause

`server-node-events` currently canonicalizes the incoming session key and then treats every node `exec.*` event the same way:

- enqueue a system event on the canonical session key
- request an `exec-event` heartbeat wake

That is correct for normal agent sessions, but it is wrong for synthetic heartbeat-isolated sessions. Those sessions already contain the inline tool/exec transcript for the current run. Re-injecting the same exec lifecycle event through the system-event queue creates an unnecessary second heartbeat run against the same synthetic session.

The result is bursty extra heartbeat traffic and avoidable token spend, especially when a heartbeat falls back to small `exec` probes such as reading local state files.

## What changed

- In `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`, load the full session entry alongside the canonical key.
- If the resolved entry has `heartbeatIsolatedBaseSessionKey`, return early for node `exec.started` / `exec.finished` / `exec.denied` handling.
- Add a focused regression test proving `exec.finished` on `agent:main:main:heartbeat` does **not** enqueue a system event or request another heartbeat wake.

## Scope boundary

This PR is intentionally narrow.

It does **not** change:

- normal node-exec notifications for non-heartbeat sessions
- isolated-heartbeat prompt construction
- isolated-heartbeat persona/bootstrap behavior
- wake coalescing or scheduler policy

It only suppresses the node-event feedback path for synthetic heartbeat sessions that already have the exec/tool transcript inline.

## Why this is distinct from nearby heartbeat fixes

This sits in the same bug family as recent isolated-heartbeat fixes, but it covers a different failure mode:

- `#59606` stabilizes repeated `:heartbeat` suffix growth after wake re-entry.
- `#67059` fixes isolated heartbeat exec prompts/persona once an exec event is already being relayed.

This PR prevents one specific class of redundant relays from being emitted in the first place.

## Validation

Ran on the PR branch:

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-node-events.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.test.src.json --noEmit`
- `pnpm check:changed --staged`

`check:changed --staged` completed successfully for this diff, including the gateway-targeted changed test plan.

target.title: fix(cli): route node status hints through stdout consistently (#83925)
target.body:
Fixes #83925.

`runNodeDaemonStatus` (`src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts:199-235`) routed service-start hints to stdout in the `!loaded` branch (matching `renderNodeServiceStartHints` and the rest of the status pretty-printer), but the two follow-up paths — `runtime.missingUnit` and `runtime.status === "stopped"` — sent `buildNodeRuntimeHints` lines to stderr instead. Users running `openclaw node status 2>/dev/null` (or any wrapper script that captures stdout for parsing) saw hints in the "never installed" path but lost them in the "installed but unit missing / stopped" paths.

## Changes

- `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts`: keep the terse machine-readable status sentence (`"Service unit not found."` / `"Service is loaded but not running."`) on `defaultRuntime.error` so wrappers that already grep stderr for those exact lines continue to work, but route the follow-up `buildNodeRuntimeHints` loop bodies through `defaultRuntime.log + infoText` — matching the `!loaded` branch convention.

Diff stat: 1 file, +8 / -2.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sanitized issue evidence — when the service has never been installed, hints reach stdout via `defaultRuntime.log`. When the service is loaded but the unit is missing or it is stopped, the same class of hint goes to stderr via `defaultRuntime.error`. The `--json` path is unaffected (returns earlier); the inconsistency only impacts plain-text consumers.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83925.mjs` (a) parses the patched `daemon.ts` and verifies the two terse status sentences still go to stderr, both hint loops now use `defaultRuntime.log + infoText`, and there are no remaining `.error(errorText(hint))` loop bodies in the function; and (b) replays the three branches (not_loaded / missing_unit / stopped) under both the patched shape (hints on stdout in all three) and the pre-fix shape (hints on stderr in missing_unit + stopped — the asymmetry the issue reports).

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm install && pnpm build && node openclaw.mjs node status` (live CLI against the local stopped systemd-user service) AND `node /tmp/probe_83925.mjs`

- **Live `openclaw node status` invocation against a real stopped service** (Linux, systemd user manager, gateway unit disabled):

```
$ node openclaw.mjs node status 2>/dev/null
Service: systemd user (disabled)
Runtime: stopped (state inactive, sub dead, last exit 0, reason 0)

Start with: openclaw node install
Start with: openclaw node start
Start with: systemctl --user start openclaw-node.service

$ node openclaw.mjs node status 1>/dev/null
(empty)
```

All three `Start with:` hint lines reach stdout in the **stopped** state — the exact path the issue reports as inconsistent vs the not_loaded branch. `2>/dev/null` consumers no longer lose hints just because the unit happens to be stopped rather than missing. The `Service:` and `Runtime:` lines are baseline status info (already on stdout by design via `defaultRuntime.log` at `daemon.ts:241,256`) — distinct from the terse status sentences (`Service unit not found.` / `Service is loaded but not running.`) which appear in the not_loaded / missing_unit branches and are intentionally kept on stderr for machine-readable consumers.

- **Probe evidence:**

```
PASS: 'Service unit not found.' status line still on stderr (machine-readable)
PASS: 'Service is loaded but not running.' status line still on stderr
PASS: both hint loops (missingUnit + stopped) route through defaultRuntime.log + infoText
PASS: no remaining .error(errorText(hint)) loop bodies (no stderr-routed hints)
PASS: replay (patched): hint lines appear on stdout in all three failure paths — consistent for `node status 2>/dev/null` consumers
PASS: replay (buggy): hints only on stderr for missing_unit/stopped — confirms the inconsistency from #83925
PASS: replay (patched): terse status sentences stay on stderr (machine consumers preserved)

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `openclaw node status 2>/dev/null` now prints the runtime hints across all three failure modes consistently. Machine-readable consumers that already filter stderr for the terse status sentence (`"Service unit not found."`, `"Service is loaded but not running."`) are unaffected — those lines stay where they were.

- **What was not tested:** Live `openclaw node status` against a real launchd/systemd/schtasks installation in each of the three failure modes — that requires platform-specific service setup. The probe replays the three branches end-to-end and verifies the source-level routing matches the existing `!loaded` branch's pattern.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses the existing `defaultRuntime.log`, `defaultRuntime.error`, `buildNodeRuntimeHints`, `errorText`, and `infoText` helpers — all of which are already in scope. No new helper. The new routing matches the `!loaded` branch that already uses `defaultRuntime.log` + `infoText` for `renderNodeServiceStartHints()`. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `runNodeDaemonStatus` has one production caller (`src/cli/node-cli/register.ts:73`). The signature is unchanged; the behavior change is presentation-only (which stream the hint lines land on). PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** `gh pr list --search '83925 in:title,body'` returns no open PRs. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -5 -- src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` shows `e1061a8b46 test(live): tolerate provider drift in release checks` — unrelated. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — display routing only.
